sentences.text: Yes.
In ghetto, before a couple months, we were without stars, without nothing.
Then, I don't remember a-- a star.
I think it was a star.
But I remember very good, later on the-- the change to a yellow badge in the front and in the back.
Yes, in the front, and in the back, because if you don't see, then at least in the back you should see.
Yes.
Who gave?
The-- the-- the-- there come a order, and the Judenrat gave this, you know, that-- to-- to wear.
And then we had the police, Jewish police, also, to-- to, you know, to stay by the-- by the-- not to go out from the ghetto, not to go, you know.
They don't--
--have no choice.
Yeah.
They-- they didn't have no choice.
No-- round, only-- and in-- in back-- in the back.
Plain yellow.
I-- I-- this, you see, I don't remember the-- that question.
You asked the first time, that-- about the-- I think it was before a-- a Jewish star.
I think so.
I don't remember-- a Jewish star only here.
And then there was--
--not good-- was not good.
Because from the time, you didn't see.
They put-- no, this is not good-- a yellow here and in the back.
Yes.
You couldn't go out with the-- if they catch you, they could kill you.
Anyway, they kill you.
[PAUSES FOR 6 SECONDS]
Can you describe to me-- you mentioned before, [NON-ENGLISH].
Action.
This-- for instance, they used to-- to lie with all kind tricks.
The first time, they told that they go to send us to Dubienka-- no, no [NON-ENGLISH]-- Wisnicz.
This is not far from-- Kiev is-- there is a-- a-- a town, Wisnicz, that they sent people to Wisnicz to work.
When they killed the people in the-- in the forest, they come to ask in the Judenrat-- Judenrat, where is the-- the people that they-- they went to work?
And the German told them, that they-- they take them to Wisnicz-- that they-- they believed.
And they say, they went to Wisnicz to work.
And then they start to-- to, you know.
And they start-- come one and two goyim.
And they told her there-- the-- the-- that she's all right, you know, the-- the-- the-- she's all right.
Shooting-- the shooting-- the shooting, and that they-- they shoot them there.
Yes.
[PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS]
Yes.
Yeah.
In the ghetto, my mother, and my brother, and my sister was also.
But she-- she didn't want to go no place.
She say, I don't want to go to work.
She didn't want to go to the [NON-ENGLISH]-- to the, you know, to Boguszow, to-- to Dolhoszyje.
She said, anyway, they'll-- either they'll kill me, then I need to work for them.
I don't want.
She was in ghetto.
She didn't want to go.
The ghetto looked very, very crowdy.

You know, this was-- they took spacial apart, where is-- where the-- the poorest people which were-- live, you know, and the-- the-- the-- in-- in-- in the-- and not so much houses-- many houses apart.
And the-- and the-- the same day, when they made the ghetto, right away, they put in from a-- a-- a [NON-ENGLISH] The shtetl is [NON-ENGLISH] is the name-- [NON-ENGLISH]-- together they brought in.
And like I tell you-- no.
This was in a one small room was living four, five families with children.
Yes.
Food was-- they-- they brought-- they brought a-- how-- a little bit food.
The-- the goyim, some-- them-- they made, you know, reserve a little bit, also the-- the shtetl of the Jewish people.
Food wasn't so bad, because they didn't have time.
It wasn't a long time, the ghetto.
The ghetto-- we say, that when '42 was the ghetto-- '41, '42, '40-- it was-- I think it was a couple months-- even not a year-- a couple months was the ghetto.
In Dolhoszyje.
And more people was taken from Judenrat.
And anyway, when-- when they come for the last action, Judenrein, to make, and when the-- the Fogel told the ran-- people ran out from here, wherever you can.
I cannot save anymore.
You go.
I cannot save any more of you.
Then the-- they ran out.
They come to the [NON-ENGLISH].
They come in all places.
And they went to the forest.
They made bunkers and everything.
But there was the [NON-ENGLISH], the Polish partisan, and the Banderas.
And that killed out all of them in the forest.
Banderas are Ukrainian-- these partisan, which want-- fight for a independent Ukraine.
The-- the-- why they-- they killed Jews for-- for pleasure, I think.
You understand me?
They took the-- the-- the Jews.
I went in a-- in a forest, Boguszow [NON-ENGLISH]-- [NON-ENGLISH], Boguszow-- [NON-ENGLISH] was a-- a-- a-- a this a village in-- in a forest, was another name-- Boguszow-- [NON-ENGLISH].
The forest.
I was there-- I, and my mother, and my brother.
This was wintertime.
It was very, very cold.
No.
We-- we escaped from the farm, from the-- from the farm we were-- where we work.
Yes.
My mother cooked there for the-- for the manager.
And we-- I helped also, and we picked up apple-- I, my brother-- and potatoes with, you know, with the--
A couple months.
And when come, then after-- before-- this is between the Yom Tovim, you know.
This was September.
Yes, like October-- October we say.
October was-- came the gestapo to kill the Jews.
And--
No.
I wasn't.
I was the farm.
I was in the farm.
Then many, many, Jews came to our farm, to other-- they came, you know.
What we should do-- we should do?
We ran out, because we-- we know that they'll come after us.
In fact, they came to-- to take us.
But we wasn't there already.
We ran out.
Then everyone ran when-- then they ran to the-- to the Boguszow forest.
And we-- we went also to Boguszow forest.
This was also almost wintertime.
And my mother went to-- we was hungry.
She went to a-- a woman, to a house, you know-- potatoes, something, you know, to eat.
We didn't eat three days-- came two Banderas and a-- and one which takes care on the-- on the forest.
How you call here the-- the one that takes care of-- on the forest?
Ranger?
A ranger-- came a ranger, and he put the-- the-- the rifle.
He say, from which ghetto are you?
I say, what-- what different is which ghetto?
Anyway, you want to-- to kill us.
Ukrainian to him.
And he say-- and-- and-- and the other one said, look.
Let them to die.
Look how they're-- they're dirty.
We were really dirty, cold.
And she was, you know, with-- with-- old schmattas, because they took away everything.
Let them to die.
Let them to-- you'll-- you'll make a-- a blessing for them.
Let them.
And they speak.
What he spoke, I feel that he was to-- want to save us.
Then the-- they say, maybe you are right.
Let them to-- to-- to-- to-- to-- to-- no-- [NON-ENGLISH] you know, like to die here, you know, in misery.
And they went away.
And he made so that we should wait.
And we waited.
He came.
And he say, look.
There is these graves, which there is Jewish people.
But you know, the Banderas, like these two, they kill them.
If you see there that nobody is there, ran out.
Because maybe they kill them or maybe they ran out.
Because there was 12 people there on the grave-- in a bunker.
We went there.
It was nobody.
If nobody's there, ran away.
And we ranned away.
And later, I'll tell you what-- how I met him.
I met him-- them there.
The ranger, yes.
[PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS]
I will tell you something.
I didn't see too much Germans.
Because like I told you, I was lucky.
Whenever was action, I was outside, you know.
And then I was in a-- in the-- in the farm.
And then when was the-- the action, the-- the end action, I wasn't there also.
I saw Fogel, and I saw the-- this-- the other.
But-- but I didn't see the-- the--
In the streets, and-- and-- and--
No.
The-- the-- I see the-- the-- the Ukrainian mostly it was.
There was-- there was terrible.
This I saw.
The-- they was so bad.
They used to beat up.
And they used to-- to-- to kill, and to-- and to rape-- ah-- all the Ukrainian.
Forest, yes.
No.
No, I didn't stay.
We went from place to place.
We went so-- or-- [NON-ENGLISH].
You know what it is.
If we didn't have no choice, then we need to go.
But we didn't know if you are-- you'll kill us or you'll give us a-- a-- at least a piece of bread.
Sometime we used to go, you know, in the morning, when the goy used to take out for the-- for the pigs the potatoes with-- with all kind-- dirty.
We used to take out to-- to steal, and ran away with these potato, with the cold potato.
And I-- I didn't get sick.
Oh, the [NON-ENGLISH].
Was our-- yes.
[NON-ENGLISH].
No.
I will tell you something.
I-- I was very religious.
I come from a very religious home.
And I was special religion.
My sister, the bigger sister, wasn't.
She was a Halutza-- Hashomer Hatzair, Halutza-- you know, more left.
And my sister, Mindel, also not so.
My brother, the youngest, I need to watch him when he daven.
And he used to, you know, to-- to jump over.
But then I-- I-- I was the one, you know, where-- where what I--
No, at home-- at home.
When we ran away, then I picked up Mondays.
I used to fast-- or Thursday-- to make, you know, a [NON-ENGLISH].
If-- if-- if I'll be still alive. then Thursday, I'll fast.
If I'm still alive, I'll be-- next Thursday.

And you know, so feelings, intuitions, my-- my girlfriend told Jaacov my intuitions were so good, that when later on-- later on, I had-- I had, you know-- when I ran from house, we had-- we had a lot of jewelry.
This gold--
Yes-- gold [NON-ENGLISH].
We-- no.
They told to bring the jewelry.
If not, they kill us.
And I was-- I said-- go to my mom, don't-- don't give them, because they'll kill anyway.
We hide them.
Maybe we'll need to ran, and we left for what to buy something.
Yes.
It was, you know, hidden.
And when we ran out from the ghetto, to-- to the-- to the-- to the-- farm, we take with us a [NON-ENGLISH], dollars, them-- this gold--
--coins, and jewelry-- and jewelry-- a full, you know.
And, you know-- and I think, OK.
You know, you have.
Maybe someday I'll give this.
And I'll give-- I'll buy a bread.
No, we need to divide, because three-- nobody wants to-- to hold us.
We divided after the-- the time when she went to take bread, and it was three people, you know.
I say, what?
To catch and to-- to kill three people-- maybe one will be alive, maybe.
And, you know.
Then--
This was-- this was when we already-- this was '43-- '40-- end of-- this was '40-- end of-- end '42 when wintertime end-- '42.
[PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS]
Then I start to go alone.
I start to go in there, go alone.
I started to go alone.
And I went here, there.
I-- I was in-- in a-- where the the cows at night.
I came in there The goy shouldn't know.
I went in-- in straw, you know.
Hidden-- wintertime, wintertime.
I went.
And I have lice, all in the head.
I-- my-- my whole skin was eaten up-- end of.
When I-- when I was-- I used to-- to take-- I had nails.
I didn't wash myself for two years.
I had big nails.
When I came on the snow-- it was black, the snow.
And I had a fur.
Everything they took away from me.
I went with schmattas on the feet.
They took away-- I had, you know, very nice boots.
And inside was this fur.
From home, I took this.
And one told me, look, anyway, they'll kill you.
I'll give you two bread, and I'll give a bottle of milk.
I think, maybe she's right.
I drink the milk.
And I ate a bread.
And she gave me two breads and a-- a bottle of milk.
And I gave her the-- the boots.
Yes.
I was for almost two years.
Yes.
No, no, no.
No, no, no, no.
Then I-- I were-- I was where we had the farm.
They looked at me.
They heard that we ran away.
And they looked at-- for us, like-- like for-- for-- what can I tell you?
For-- for a big-- because they were afraid they will be--
--no.
If we be-- will alive, we'll take away the farm and everything, the-- you understand me?
Then the-- we couldn't come there.
We went in a different-- where we went there nobody knows us, you know-- and only the manager.
Then we-- we went there from strange people-- strange people.
Then I-- I remember that we had a shiksa that she was a-- a Baptist.
Then I think, oye, if I could reach maybe a Baptist, or maybe he could help me, because they're a very religious people.
But how I can know?
You know, like-- Where-- where did she live?
Where, the shiksa?
No, the shiksa is gone already.
No, no, no.
We had once a shiksa, where--
--that she-- a-- knew-- a-- a maid.
And she's gone.
But she was very good, you know.
They didn't steal.
There was a-- she was speaking all day with God.
Yes.
Maybe would help me.
But I-- I how I know where is Baptist and where is a Bandera?
Then I went and went, and so many times, you know, I-- I almost was killed.
And then, like, somebody-- I didn't have where to go.
It was winter, snow.
And I took out the snow, and I pull out the--
--the lice, excuse me.
And here from the--
--from the fur.
And I put the fur.
It was black, you know.
So they was eating alive, me.
What can I do?
Then I-- I stood by-- there was a small-- [NON-ENGLISH]-- you know, a small [NON-ENGLISH] between a-- Dolhoszyje and-- and [NON-ENGLISH], you know?
It was-- then I-- I was waiting.
They were [NON-ENGLISH] the-- the [NON-ENGLISH].
I was waiting.
And they were-- and-- and I don't know why.
People were going and coming.
And I was waiting until everyone go.
I don't know why.
I was so-- no-- convinced that I need to go there.
I came there, you know.
And he went.
He say, wait a moment.
Go in the-- in the-- in the shire-- shire.
I went there.
And I was waiting.
He say, now nobody's there.
I think, oye my, God.
Maybe he wants to kill me, you know.
I came in.
A-- a big man, you know-- and he called me in.
They called me in.
I saw maybe six children, or seven children.
And he gave me right away a-- a glass milk-- warm milk and a piece of bread.
And on the-- on the pitch, you know, where you bake bread wintertime, the children go there to make warm.
And I saw one child, like-- like a Jewish child, like another's child.
But I didn't say nothing.
Then he says, you know why-- why you are this-- new light suffer so much?
I said, because we didn't believe in Jesus Christ.
You know?
I thought, maybe this will help me.
Oh!
He-- now, I read that they wanted to convert for Jesus Christ, people.
Then he-- Do you remember the name of this gentleman that took you in?
Yes.
Ah, the name?
One moment-- Homa-- Homa-- Homak-- Homa-- but the second name, I don't remember.
I stood there-- I didn't stay.
I was one night.
One moment.
I was one night.
And then came a boy, older to him, and he gave him a glass milk with bread.
And he told, we should go in the shire to-- to sleep.
Then we already-- no, there was still my mother and brother, yes.
Yes.
And he was--
--and he was-- he was our [NON-ENGLISH].
I-- I woke up in the morning.
I don't have his gun.
I don't have the--
--no.
This, don't-- don't write this.
Don't write this.
I lost this.
And when I lost the gold, then start-- you know, when I had the gold, it was terrible.
Every time something happened to me, and they're looking for me to kill me.
When I think, my God, what I'll I do?
I lost the gold and everything.
And if I would sell somebody a piece of gold, they would kill me, because I have gold.
Yes.
And then I-- I-- he came in the morning.
I was still there.
He called us in.
And he say, look.
Three, I cannot hold.
I can only take you in the meantime.
And I can.
And my mother and brother went.
Yes.
My mother I found-- we-- we met like couple time-- but after-- after the-- when the-- the-- after the liberation.
The Russian.
In a-- in a village.
Dolhoszyje.
The date-- I-- I think it was the 9th of-- 9th of February, '44.
[PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS]
'44-- February '44 is for sure.
But the 9th, or the 11th, I don't remember.
Yes.
No, when I was-- when-- when they come, already-- Russian, I was liberated.
That I know already then.
I didn't know the track, no.
I didn't know the time.
I didn't know-- they didn't know either, the goyim.
I don't think that they know.
No.
No.
Yes.
The day of liberation, we-- from this goy, what he told me, the Ukrainian--
--that he cannot-- Homa.
They started, you know-- he had under the-- a-- a teacher from Radum-- hiding.
His home.
He had a-- a-- a Jewish child on the top.
His wife died on typhus, because the lice-- not me.
I-- when I was, she was dead already.
And he was hiding-- was afraid, because his brother, and his uncle, and his-- his neighbors was all Bandera.
So he was afraid-- afraid.
Once I was there, you know.
And they came at night.
They beat up so his daughter.
They thought that this is me, you know?
If-- then the-- the brother, the-- his brother told him-- so, this is-- he say, what you-- you are crazy?
He recognized her.
They called him.
He say, no.
This is my niece.
Leave her alone.
So they were looking for you.
He told.
He told that a Jewish-- he told.
But then they called her, he should recognize.
He said, my God.
This is my-- my-- my niece!
And then he told, you cannot come anymore.
But I'll speak, you know, I'll speak with my friends.
And then the-- they'll already start, you know.
And I came to this [NON-ENGLISH].
And there we were a whole year-- maybe-- maybe less than a year.
Ah, liberation day.
Then we were-- it was very cold, wintertime.
He had a nice house.
And we were all the time, you know, in the house and then in the-- in the-- he started-- we shouldn't freeze anymore, you know.
And at night, he thought, you know-- and-- and they used to go-- the partisans-- the Soviet partisans-- they used to go through the villages, you know?
And-- and they used to say, you know, [NON-ENGLISH].
It means the-- the bad people, you know, the-- like nuts.
And-- and he used to-- and-- go-- go on the-- on the-- on the pitch.
Hide yourself.
We hiding in the pitch.
And we go in, you know.
Two soldiers, you know-- and we saw that this is not-- not Ukraine police.
They speak a perfect Russian.
And I was looking so.
And I say, Sonya-- I was already with the Canadian friend, Sonya.
I say, this is not a-- a partisan.
No, this-- this is not a Bandera.
This is a-- this a-- a Russian.
And he say, come.
I-- the-- the-- I need horses.
I need horses.
He came out, the [NON-ENGLISH].
We called him uncle.
He came out.
He saw so many.
He say-- and he was a communist.
He was, you know, a socialist.
He say-- he was-- he was drinking.
He was a alcoholic.
Then he-- when they-- they threw him out from the Baptist.
No.
He didn't tell.
We right away, we understood.
We right away understand.
